scenarios.name: a member can acknowledge an announcement only once

steps.sql: INSERT INTO app_announcements__announcements (id, title, body, status, author_id, author_name, expires_at, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'approved', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
INSERT INTO app_announcements__acknowledgements (announcement_id, member_id, acknowledged_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)
INSERT INTO app_announcements__acknowledgements (announcement_id, member_id, acknowledged_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)

scenarios.name: only the author edits their announcement; approvals require the moderator group

steps.sql: INSERT INTO app_announcements__announcements (id, title, body, status, author_id, author_name, expires_at, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'pending', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
UPDATE app_announcements__announcements SET title = 'hijacked' WHERE id = ?
SELECT title FROM app_announcements__announcements WHERE id = ?
INSERT INTO app_announcements__approvals (announcement_id, approved_by, approved_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)

scenarios.name: the email-everyone flag is written by its author and readable back

steps.sql: INSERT INTO app_announcements__announcements (id, title, body, status, author_id, author_name, expires_at, created_at, updated_at, is_critical, email_everyone) VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'pending', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0, 1)
SELECT email_everyone FROM app_announcements__announcements WHERE id = ?
UPDATE app_announcements__announcements SET email_everyone = 1 WHERE id = ?
SELECT email_everyone FROM app_announcements__announcements WHERE id = ?
